board.designation: DB-EM — Emergency lighting central battery sub-DB, IEC commercial 3-storey office

incoming_supply.fed_from: MSB-MAIN F05 (40A MCB Type C, 80m feeder, NO upstream RCD per IEC 60364-5-56:2018 §560.7)

selectivity_results.code_clause: IEC 60364-5-53 Clause 536 — 40A Type C MCB upstream selective against ≤16A Type C MCBs at DB-EM (2.5:1 minimum ratio met for EM07; 4:1+ ratio for EM01-EM06 and EM08 supports time-current selectivity per IEC 60898 cascade). Engineer-declared per manufacturer cascade table at 7.5 kA fault level.

non_compliance_flags.message: DB-EM carries emergency-lighting life-safety load — BS EN 50171:2001+A1:2022 requires 3-hour central-battery autonomy minimum and IEC 60598-2-22:2014+A2:2020 governs luminaire conformity. AHJ-required survival duration (1h, 2h, or 3h per local building code) must be verified against the battery commissioning certificate; central-battery installer to provide IEC 61851/EN 50171 compliance certificate and 3-hour discharge test report at handover.

Neutral current 5.0 A is 48.1% of the largest phase (L1 = 10.4 A). Per IEC 60364-1 § 132.6: single-phase load distribution best practice — rebalance recommended. Re-allocate one or more circuits between phases to reduce neutral loading and improve voltage symmetry.

assumptions: DB-EM is the emergency-lighting central-battery sub-DB — `role: emergency_lighting_panel` flagged in the board block.

IEC 60364-5-56:2018 §560 (life-safety/standby supply systems) governs the entire EM distribution: NO upstream RCD on essential circuits, dedicated circuit isolation, fire-rated cables, secondary supply integration.
ALL circuits use `voltage_class: "emergency_lighting"` to signal segregation requirements to downstream consumers (riser, cable-containment, segregation-distance constraints).

Central-battery system designed per BS EN 50171:2001+A1:2022 — 3-hour autonomy minimum, sealed VRLA or Li-ion battery bank with integral charger and changeover inverter; complete unit listed as IEC 60598-2-22 compliant emergency-lighting power supply.

Luminaires throughout (corridor escape route + stairwell + exit signs) conform to IEC 60598-2-22:2014+A2:2020 — self-contained vs centrally-fed addressed by the central-battery topology here; all luminaires fed at 230V from the EM panel via fire-rated cable.

FP200 LSZH fire-rated cable specified for all EM final circuits — provides 30/60/120-minute fire survival depending on cable class (per IEC 60331 / BS EN 50200) — essential because EM luminaires must remain energised during a fire to illuminate escape routes.

NO RCD anywhere in the DB-EM chain (including upstream at MSB-MAIN F05) per IEC 60364-5-56:2018 §560.7 — earth-fault trip on a life-safety EM circuit during fire-induced cable degradation would extinguish escape-route lighting at the exact moment occupants depend on it.
Estimated PFC at DB-EM ≈ 7.5 kA after 80m 16mm² Cu XLPE submain impedance from 22.5 kA at MSB-MAIN; 10 kA Icn MCBs cover this without cascade backup.
All Type C MCBs (Ia = 10×In) — handles inrush of LED EM luminaire driver banks + battery-charger SMPS energisation.

EM07 battery charger circuit sized 16A Type C to handle the central-battery initial charging current after a discharge cycle; downstream load 1.2 kW corresponds to a ~5A steady-state charging current with headroom for boost-charge transients.

drawing_notes: DB-EM located in the ground-floor EM equipment room, adjacent to MSB-MAIN for short feeder run and maintenance access — co-located with the central battery cabinet to minimise EM-side cabling.
Fed from MSB-MAIN F05 via 80m 16mm² 5-core Cu XLPE submain (FP200 alternative possible if the feeder route passes through unprotected escape routes — engineer to confirm fire-strategy zoning).
8 circuits + 4 spare ways (W9-W12 reserved — typical future expansion includes basement EM zone, plant-room EM, external escape-route luminaires, or addressable luminaire-monitoring interface).
Role: emergency_lighting_panel — flagged in board block for downstream SLD/riser/segregation consumers.
NO RCD upstream or at DB-EM per IEC 60364-5-56:2018 §560.7.
ALL final-circuit cables FP200 LSZH for fire survival per IEC 60331 — EM luminaires must remain illuminated during fire-induced cable degradation.
Central battery sized for 3-hour autonomy minimum per BS EN 50171 §6.12; AHJ may require shorter (1h) or longer (3h+) per local building code — verify at handover.

rationale.chat_summary: DB-EM: 40A TPN emergency-lighting central-battery sub-DB, 8 circuits (6 EM lighting zones + charger + monitoring), fed from MSB-MAIN F05 with NO upstream RCD per IEC 60364-5-56:2018 §560.7. Central battery rated 3-hour autonomy per BS EN 50171; luminaires per IEC 60598-2-22. All Type C MCBs, FP200 LSZH cable throughout. INFO flag: AHJ survival duration to be verified at handover.

sections.title: Board Identification
sections.summary: DB-EM is the dedicated emergency-lighting sub-DB for the IEC 3-storey commercial office, located in the ground-floor EM equipment room adjacent to MSB-MAIN. Role flagged as `emergency_lighting_panel` so downstream SLD, riser, and cable-containment skills recognise it as a life-safety distribution.

decisions.label: Board role flagged as emergency_lighting_panel
decisions.summary: Signals life-safety distribution to all downstream skills (SLD, riser, segregation).

decisions.label: Location: ground-floor EM equipment room
decisions.summary: Co-located with central battery cabinet and adjacent to MSB-MAIN to minimise feeder length and ease maintenance access.

sections.title: Incoming Supply
sections.summary: 40A TPN intake fed from MSB-MAIN F05 via 80m 16mm² Cu XLPE submain. PFC attenuated from ~22.5 kA at MSB-MAIN to ~7.5 kA at DB-EM by the submain impedance — within 10 kA Icn of standard MCBs. NO upstream RCD per §560.7 life-safety exemption.

decisions.label: Supply class: essential
decisions.summary: DB-EM feeds life-safety EM luminaires only.

decisions.label: NO upstream RCD on essential circuits
decisions.summary: Earth-fault trip during fire-induced cable degradation would extinguish escape-route lighting.

decisions.label: Declared PFC 7.5 kA after 80m submain
decisions.summary: Cable-Z attenuated from 22.5 kA at MSB-MAIN; covered by standard 10 kA Icn MCBs without cascade backup.

sections.title: Circuit Breakdown
sections.summary: 8 final circuits arranged as 6 EM lighting zones (3 corridor/escape-route + 3 stairwell/exit-sign across GF, L1, L2) plus EM07 battery-charger and EM08 monitoring/test. Corridor zones loaded ~0.8 kW each (LED escape-route luminaires); stairwell zones ~0.4 kW (lower density). EM07 battery charger sized 16A Type C for the central-battery boost-charge transient; EM08 monitoring is a low-current interface circuit. Cable lengths step up by floor as expected.

decisions.label: All Type C MCBs
decisions.summary: Handles LED driver inrush and battery-charger SMPS energisation; tighter than Type D for earth-fault disconnection.
decisions.rule: MCB curve selection for SMPS/electronic loads

decisions.label: 6A for stairwell + monitoring; 10A for corridor; 16A for charger
decisions.summary: Sized to nameplate steady-state load plus 25% headroom for inrush, avoiding nuisance trip on LED driver energisation.

decisions.label: FP200 LSZH 1.5/2.5mm² Cu throughout
decisions.summary: Fire-survival cable to keep EM luminaires energised during cable degradation in a fire.

sections.title: Selectivity Analysis
sections.summary: Upstream MSB-MAIN F05 = 40A Type C MCB. Downstream MCBs at DB-EM are 6-16A Type C. Worst-case ratio at EM07 (16A) = 2.5:1 — meets the minimum threshold for IEC 60898 same-curve cascade time-current selectivity at 7.5 kA fault level. Remaining circuits operate at 4:1+ ratio with comfortable selectivity margin. Verdict pass — engineer-declared per manufacturer cascade table.

decisions.label: 40A:16A worst-case ratio = 2.5:1
decisions.summary: Meets minimum same-curve cascade threshold; manufacturer cascade table confirms selectivity to 7.5 kA.

decisions.label: Engineer-declared per manufacturer table
decisions.summary: Same-curve Type C cascade requires manufacturer-specific confirmation; defer to fault-level skill for full coordination study.

sections.title: Compliance Assessment
sections.summary: DB-EM is compliant against IEC 60364-5-56:2018 §560 (life-safety supply chain), BS EN 50171:2001+A1:2022 (central-battery system), and IEC 60598-2-22:2014+A2:2020 (luminaire conformity). One INFO flag: AHJ-required survival duration (1h / 2h / 3h per local building code) must be verified against the battery commissioning certificate at handover. Central-battery installer to provide BS EN 50171 compliance certificate + 3-hour discharge test report.

decisions.label: Central battery rated 3-hour autonomy minimum
decisions.summary: BS EN 50171 §6.12 specifies minimum autonomy; AHJ may require longer per local building code.

decisions.label: Luminaires conform to IEC 60598-2-22
decisions.summary: Escape-route + stairwell + exit-sign luminaires fed at 230V from central battery via fire-rated cable; all listed as IEC 60598-2-22 compliant.

decisions.label: INFO flag — AHJ survival duration verification
decisions.summary: Not a non-compliance; design-decision flag requiring handover-time confirmation by AHJ + installer.

sections.title: Schedule Notes
sections.summary: Schedule emitted as 8-row table with 4 spare ways (W9-W12) for future expansion. Spare-way reservation aligns with typical EM-system growth patterns: basement EM zone, plant-room EM coverage, external escape-route luminaires, or addressable luminaire-monitoring interface. NO RCD across the entire DB-EM chain — life-safety exemption flagged on every row.

decisions.label: 4 spare ways reserved
decisions.summary: Typical EM-system expansion: basement, plant-room, external escape routes, or addressable monitoring interface.

invariants.evidence: INV-14 board DB-EM (INT → IEC): R1 jurisdiction→format mapping (INT/EU → IEC 60364-5-51 §514) holds; R2 board_label.text+svg populated, no {{}} remnants; R3 board_label.text matches IEC format regex; R4 8/8 circuits carry circuit_label.text+svg, no {{}} remnants; R5 every circuit_label.text matches IEC circuit regex; R6 tool_call_pending booleans set on all labels.
